summary: Fixed the GitHub Actions EditorConfig check after an upstream nixpkgs/editorconfig-checker packaging breakage by removing a separate nix-env install step and invoking editorconfig-checker through nix-shell directly in the check command. The PR was a small CI repair in .github/workflows/editorconfig.yml, with discussion about whether to keep relying on Nix, pin versions, or move to a container/hash-based tool download.
what_changed: Removed the separate GitHub Actions step named "install editorconfig-checker" that ran `nix-env -iA editorconfig-checker -f '<nixpkgs>'`.

Changed the EditorConfig check command from piping changed files directly into `editorconfig-checker` to running the check inside `nix-shell -p editorconfig-checker --run 'xargs -r editorconfig-checker -disable-indent-size'`.
Kept the existing pinned nixpkgs tarball configuration for editorconfig-checker 2.4.0.
smart_moves: Avoided relying on a globally installed editorconfig-checker from `nix-env`; the tool is instead made available in the same command context that runs the check.
Kept the fix minimal: one workflow file changed, with one added line and three deleted lines.
Applied an upstream nixpkgs-related fix referenced in the PR body: https://github.com/NixOS/nixpkgs/commit/31ce09b37790915618e839ed6a728f7b40157fdc.
decomp_lessons: CI formatting checks are part of decompilation hygiene: small workflow breakages can block otherwise unrelated matching/decomp work and should be fixed with minimal, reviewable changes.
When a CI tool is provided by a package manager, prefer invocation patterns that make the tool availability explicit at the point of use.
Pinned dependencies reduce surprise, but the PR discussion shows that pinned package-manager inputs can still require maintenance when upstream tooling or packaging behavior changes.
naming_conventions: Workflow step name remained human-readable as "Checking EditorConfig".
The workflow uses `$HOME/changed_files` as the file list consumed by the EditorConfig checker.
The EditorConfig command uses the existing `-disable-indent-size` flag.
review_feedback: ribbanya initially noted the attempted fix did not work and expressed a preference for using a container to avoid future package-manager breakage.
ribbanya said the PR was running master’s workflow and therefore was difficult to test directly.
ribbanya referenced alternative PR #747 and said they were going with that approach at one point.
r-burns argued that this PR's change was tiny and would fix the issue.
r-burns suggested pinning to a specific version as another option, linking a home-manager commit as an example.
ribbanya objected to relying on Nix for CI and preferred a hash-pinned downloadable executable as more understandable and durable.
r-burns characterized the issue as a small breakage and said the fix was found by looking at upstream nixpkgs.
No inline review comments were present in the provided slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replaced the EditorConfig workflow with a container or downloaded executable.
Check PR #747 for the alternative approach discussed in this PR.
Search for future changes to .github/workflows/editorconfig.yml involving nix-shell, nix-env, or editorconfig-checker.
Audit other workflows that install tools through nix-env and could be simplified to run through nix-shell.
